channel.title: Augmented Reality | TMU Multimedia Research Laboratory

item.title: Intelligent Scene Understanding for Mobile Augmented Reality
item.description:
Overview

This project develops machine learning algorithms for 3D object recognition using point cloud data captured from ARKit and ARCore frameworks, enabling intelligent scene understanding and collaborative augmented reality experiences on mobile devices. In partnership with AWE Company, the work targets real-world AR applications that can semantically understand their environment in real time.

Collaborators

  • AWE Company

Funding

  • NSERC Collaborative Research and Development Grant (CRD)

item.title: Augmented Reality Framework for Museum Exhibits
item.description:
Overview

In partnership with SimentIT and the Canadian Science and Technology Museum (CSTM) in Ottawa, this project developed a cloud-based mobile augmented reality platform for museum exhibits. The system enables real-time 3D object recognition and pose estimation under challenging and variable museum lighting conditions. The technology was deployed in CSTM’s “Artifact Alley”, where visitors experience a barrier-free and natural AR experience overlaid on physical artifacts.

Collaborators

  • SimentIT
  • Canadian Science and Technology Museum (CSTM), Ottawa

Funding

  • NSERC Collaborative Research and Development Grant (CRD) — $450K
